---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 9: Convex Optimization and Gradient Methods
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Convex Optimization and Gradient Methods
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Analyze convex objectives and first-order solvers.

## 03. Recap from Unit 8
- Loss + regularization objective forms.

## 04. Optimization problem setup
- Variables, objective, constraints.

## 05. Convex sets intuition
- Feasible region geometry.

## 06. Convex functions intuition
- Global structure benefits.

## 07. First-order condition
- Gradient and stationary points.

## 08. Second-order condition (light)
- Hessian and curvature.

## 09. Why convexity matters
- No bad local minima.

## 10. Common convex losses in ML
- Least squares, logistic loss.

## 11. Regularized convex objectives
- Ridge/Lasso forms.

## 12. Gradient descent algorithm
- Iterative update rule.

## 13. Learning rate role
- Stability vs speed.

## 14. Convergence intuition
- Smooth convex setting.

## 15. Lipschitz gradient concept
- Step-size guarantees.

## 16. Strong convexity intuition
- Faster convergence behavior.

## 17. Gradient descent failure modes
- Divergence and oscillation.

## 18. Feature scaling impact
- Conditioning effects.

## 19. Condition number intuition
- Geometry of optimization difficulty.

## 20. Momentum methods overview
- Heavy-ball / Nesterov intuition.

## 21. Stochastic gradient descent
- Noisy but scalable updates.

## 22. Mini-batch tradeoffs
- Variance vs throughput.

## 23. Learning-rate schedules
- Decay and warmup concepts.

## 24. Adaptive methods (AdaGrad/Adam)
- Coordinate-wise scaling.

## 25. Proximal gradient idea
- Handling non-smooth penalties.

## 26. L1 optimization intuition
- Soft-thresholding behavior.

## 27. Constrained optimization basics
- Projection methods.

## 28. Lagrangian perspective (intro)
- Constraint incorporation.

## 29. KKT conditions (conceptual)
- Optimality with constraints.

## 30. Duality intuition
- Primal-dual viewpoints.

## 31. Line search strategies
- Practical step-size tuning.

## 32. Stopping criteria
- Gradient norm / objective gap.

## 33. Numerical stability concerns
- Precision and overflow.

## 34. Debugging optimization
- Diagnostic plots/checks.

## 35. Non-convex preview
- Deep learning bridge.

## 36. Generalization link
- Optimization vs test performance.

## 37. Implicit bias of optimizers
- Which solutions are selected.

## 38. Materials example: convex regression fit
- Practical use case.

## 39. Materials example: constrained design variable fit
- Feasibility-aware optimization.

## 40. Materials example: sparse model via L1
- Interpretability connection.

## 41. Common exam traps
- Convexity/optimality confusions.

## 42. Checklist for solver choice
- Objective + scale + constraints.

## 43. Exercise 1 scaffold
- GD on least-squares objective.

## 44. Exercise 2 scaffold
- SGD vs GD comparison.

## 45. Exercise 3 scaffold
- L1 proximal gradient mini-task.

## 46. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 47. Summary table
- Methods and assumptions.

## 48. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Generalization/model selection bridge.

## 50. References + transition
- Unit 10 link.

::: {#refs}
:::
